paragraph: Single-family homes in East Rutherford are moving at a median of $750,000 with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.00 and a median days on market of 17, which means sellers are getting their asking price and homes are leaving the market at a steady clip. About 57 percent of single-family sales close above list, a sign that priced homes are moving and buyers are willing to compete for the right property. Multi-family properties, by contrast, tell a stronger story. The median sits at $990,000 with every sale closing above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.12, putting this category firmly in a seller's market. Days on market average 15, suggesting real demand and limited inventory. Condo and townhouse inventory rounds out the market at a median of $560,000 with a ratio of 1.00 and a median of 24 days on market. One-third of these units sold above asking, indicating that while the category is steady, it lacks the heat of the multi-family tier. If you're weighing East Rutherford, the multi-family segment is where sellers hold the advantage right now, while single-family and condo inventory are moving at a balanced pace.
